name: Folder Size Explorer
shortDescription: A Windows storage analysis tool that shows what is taking space across folders, drives, and file trees.
longDescription: Folder Size Explorer helps Windows users, technicians, and system administrators quickly understand disk usage by scanning folders and showing where storage is being consumed. It is designed to make cleanup decisions easier by highlighting large folders, file groups, and space-heavy areas of the filesystem.

name: Folder Mover
shortDescription: A fast Windows copy and move utility for large folder jobs, network shares, and NTFS permission-aware migrations with inheritance-preserving ACL support.
longDescription: Folder Mover helps Windows users, technicians, and domain administrators move or copy large folder trees with resumable sessions, detailed logging, and NTFS permission handling. Version 1.1.0 adds Preserve Inheritance mode for server and domain migrations where the destination folder tree must keep the source permission model instead of flattening inherited source permissions into explicit entries on every destination item.

screenshots.alt: Folder Mover showing the Preserve Inheritance NTFS permission mode selected during a completed copy operation.
screenshots.caption: Preserve Inheritance mode keeps the source ACL inheritance structure across folder levels during copy and move jobs.

screenshots.alt: Folder Mover showing a completed move operation with 52,008 files moved and 8.88 GB processed.
screenshots.caption: Completed high-volume move with 52,008 files, 225 folders, and 8.88 GB processed.

screenshots.alt: Folder Mover showing a network unavailable warning with the session saved for resume.
screenshots.caption: Fault-tolerant network handling saves the session so the job can resume after reconnecting.

featureHeading: Built for interrupted real-world file moves
featureIntro: Focused controls make large local and network folder operations easier to restart, preserve, verify, and finish.
features: Copy or move folders while keeping source data intact until operations finish safely.
Resume interrupted sessions after network share failures or disconnected destinations.
Preserve NTFS permissions with options for none, protected source-only permissions, Preserve Inheritance, or inherited merges.
Preserve ACL inheritance structure by copying explicit permissions at each file and folder level and letting child items inherit from their copied destination parent.
Use multithreaded file operations for large folder trees and network migrations.
Record detailed progress, error logs, file counts, moved data size, and operation duration.
Choose practical overwrite behavior for existing files during repeat or recovery runs.
changelog: Version 1.1.0 adds Preserve Inheritance, a new NTFS permission mode for reliable server and domain folder migrations.
Preserve Inheritance copies explicit permissions at each file and folder level, then allows child items to inherit from their copied destination parent.
Merge with Inherited remains available when you want to keep the destination parent's permissions and add source effective permissions explicitly on top.

olderVersions.notes: Initial public Windows release with copy and move modes, NTFS permission options, resumable sessions, multithreaded operation, and detailed error logs.

name: Jackery Monitor
shortDescription: A self-hosted Linux monitor for Jackery portable power stations with charts, Kasa smart-plug charging automation, and a local web dashboard.
longDescription: Jackery Monitor is an unofficial self-hosted utility for Jackery portable power stations. It polls the Jackery cloud API, logs readings to CSV or MySQL, controls TP-Link Kasa smart plugs for automated charging, keeps supported device displays awake, and serves a local web dashboard with charts, device controls, configuration, and HTTP API endpoints for home automation.

screenshots.alt: Jackery Monitor dashboard with battery chart, charging controls, device status, and battery drop events.
screenshots.caption: Dashboard with live device state, charge controls, chart history, and charging overlays.

screenshots.alt: Jackery Monitor configuration page with Jackery account settings and per-device options.
screenshots.caption: Runtime configuration for Jackery account credentials, charge thresholds, and screen timeout.

changelog: Initial Linux release is available from the public GitHub repository.
Includes systemd services, web dashboard, Kasa charging automation, MySQL chart storage, and HTTP API endpoints.

name: Shared Folder Permission Viewer
shortDescription: A Windows NTFS permission auditing utility for scanning shared folders and reviewing explicit, inherited, orphaned, and risky access entries.
longDescription: Shared Folder Permission Viewer helps Windows administrators inspect NTFS permissions across shared folders, nested directory trees, local accounts, Active Directory users and groups, and orphaned SIDs. It combines a searchable permission explorer with focused security analysis views for inheritance overrides, deny rules, elevated privileges, unauthorized entries, and account lookups.

screenshots.alt: Shared Folder Permission Viewer showing a completed recursive scan with folders, ACE counts, account classification, and the permission explorer tree.
screenshots.caption: Recursive scan results show folder counts, ACE totals, AD identities, local and builtin accounts, and orphaned SID coverage.

screenshots.alt: Shared Folder Permission Viewer showing the Security Analysis tab with inheritance override findings.
screenshots.caption: Security analysis views highlight inheritance overrides, privilege findings, deny rules, unauthorized entries, orphaned SIDs, and user search results.

screenshots.alt: HTML Folder Permission Report showing audit context, filter controls, summary tiles, and a risk overview.
screenshots.caption: HTML reports include audit context, searchable filters, summary tiles, and prioritized risk overview sections.

screenshots.alt: HTML Folder Permission Report showing an expandable folder tree with explicit and inherited ACEs.
screenshots.caption: Expandable folder-tree reporting keeps large scans browsable while preserving explicit and inherited ACE detail.

screenshots.alt: HTML Folder Permission Report showing identity reference cards with group membership and folder access details.
screenshots.caption: Identity reference cards connect groups, nested members, and folder access into a reviewable report.

screenshots.alt: Microsoft Excel showing a Shared Folder Permission Viewer export with identity inventory and group member rows.
screenshots.caption: Excel exports provide tabular identity inventory, group membership, raw ACEs, findings, and scan error worksheets.

featureIntro: Focused views make permission scans easier to search, analyze, export, and revisit.
features: Scan shared folders recursively and collect NTFS access control entries into a searchable permission tree.
Separate Active Directory users and groups from local and builtin accounts during scans.
Highlight inherited permissions, explicit overrides, deny rules, elevated privileges, unauthorized entries, and orphaned SIDs.
Search by folder path or identity to trace where a user or group has access.
Export audit results to HTML and Excel for review, filtering, sharing, and offline evidence.
Use multithreaded scans for larger folder trees and file server shares.
Store scan sessions locally so previous audit results can be reopened for review.
changelog: Initial public Windows release is available as a GitHub Release download.
Includes recursive NTFS permission scanning, local and Active Directory account classification, searchable permission exploration, security analysis reports, and HTML/Excel export screenshots.
